titleStmt.title: SII 25.124: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 124. (A.R. No. 124 of 1908.) TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.

summary.p: South wall of the first prākāra in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.

p.hi: Jaṭāvarmaṉ Vikrama Pāṇḍya Year 8+14: Śaka 1344 = 1422 A.D.

summary.p: The details of the date of the record viz., year 8+14, Śaka 1344, Śubhakrit, Dhanus 19, śu 3, Wednesday, Tiruvōṇam regularly correspond to 1422 A.D., December 16.

It records the sale of uḻavu-tavaṇai (right to cultivate lands for lease) pertaining to the lands at Muḷaikkāḍu below the Muḷaikkāḍuchannel, a dēvadāṉa of the deity Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār, north of Tirupputtūr and the settlements in the west, that was lying below and not fetching the lease rent due from Aḻagiya-nambiyār Pallavarāyar, to some persons including Śeṇbagarāyar Parākrama-pāṇḍya Viśaiyālayadēvar Ariyanāyar of Śūraikkuḍi in Tēṉāṟṟuppōkku in Adaḷaiyūr-nāḍu, for reclamation cultivation and payment of the lease rent regularly, after receiving the price-value of 200 Āḍūr-paṇam from them by the Śrīrudra-śrīmāhēśvaras, śrīkāriyam-śeyvār, māḍāpattiyam, Abhimuttīsvaram-uḍaiyāṉ and dēvarkaṉmi of the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṉda-nāyaṉār at Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅgavaḷanāḍu. A tax called kīḻvārapachchai which was collected for the above lands previously was not collected later, probably after this transaction. The transaction pertaining to the lease of the temple lands was attested by the Vaḷḷalār, Aḻagapperumāḷ-nambi, dēvarkaṉmi Poṉṉa-baṭṭar and Abhimuttīśvaramuḍaiyāṉ.
The temple accountant Perumāṇḍāṉ Mayilēṟuvāṉ alias Brahmapriyaṉ is stated to have drafted the sale-deed. The inscription gives in detail the situation of the land and its boundaries.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . koccaṭaipaṉmarāṉa tribhuvaṉaccakravattikaḷ śrī vikramapāṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu 8 vatiṉ Etir 10 4āvatu
śakābdam 1000 3 100 4 10 4 mel cellāniṉṟa śubhakiṟitu varuṣam dhanuraviyat § pūrvvapakṣattu tritikaiyum putavāramum peṟṟa tiruvoṇattu
nāḷ keraḷaciṅkavaḷa nāṭṭu brahmadeyan=tirupputtūr Uṭaiyār tiruttaḷiyāṇṭa nāyiṉār koyil śrī rudraśrī māheśvaraṣa śrī kāriyañ ceyvārkaḷum
māṭāpattiyam Abhimuttiśvaramuṭaiyaāṉum tevarkaṉmiyum Ataḷaiyūr nāṭṭu teṉāṟṟup pokku cūraikkuṭi ceṇpakarāyar parākramapāṇṭiya
vicaiyālaiya tevar Ariyarāyar Uḷḷiṭṭāṟku Uḻavu tavaṇai vilai pramāṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu Ivvūr vaṭavāyvayal maḻaikkaṇṇa
vārttu Uṭaiyār tiruttaḷiyāṇṭa nāyiṉār tevatāṉam muḷaikkāṭṭuvāykkāl kiḻ muḷaikkāṭṭuvayal nāṉkellaik kuṭpaṭṭa nila
1. This word tevarkaṉmi in written below the line.

muṟṟukkum perunāṉkellaiyāvatu kiḻpāṟkellai Iṉṉāyiṉār tevatāṉam tiruppāṟkaṭal vayakkal melai vāykkālukkum kuṭikkā
ṇi Empal taṭi Iraṇṭukkum cikaṇṭaṉ vayakkal taṭi Iraṇṭukkum Itaṉ teṉ meṉ muṭalmuṟkkat tuṭa valukkum meṟkum teṉṉellai pāṇṭaṅ
kālvāykkālukku vaṭakkum melellai peruvaḻikku vaṭakku nokkippoṉa muḷaikkāṭṭu vāykkālukku kiḻakkum vaṭavellai Ivvā
ykkālukku teṟkum Āka Icainta perunāṉkellaik kuṭpaṭṭa nilamum melaikkuṭi Iruppum muṉpe Aḻakiya nampiyār pallavarāyaṟku viṟṟu Ivar vāra
mum tārāmal poy vayalum pāḻkiṭakkaiyāle Inta Ariyarāyar Uḷḷiṭṭāṟku Uḻavu tavaṇai vilaiyāka Ivvayalukku Emmilicainta vilai niṟcayitta
Āṭūr ø2 100 Ippaṇam Irunūṟum vilai Āvaṇak kaḷariyile kāṭṭi Eṟṟik kaiccelavaṟap paṟṟi vilaikkuṟa viṟṟu poruḷuṟak koṇṭu vi
ṟṟu vilai pramāṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta Ivayal cantrātittya varai Uḻutu vāramiṭuvārkaḷākavum Ippaṭi cammatittu Uḻavu tavaṇai pramā
ṇam paṇṇikkuṭuttom kiḻvārappaccai Ivvayalukku muṉpu Iṟuttup potāmaiyāl piṉpu kiḻvārap paccaik koḷḷakkaṭavatal
la vākavum Ippaṭi cammatittu Uḻavutavaṇai vilai pramāṇam paṇṇikkuṭuttom Ariyarāyar Uḷḷiṭṭāṟku cikāriyañ ceyvārkaḷum
Abhimuttiśvaramuṭaiyāṉum muppatu vaṭṭattu tevakaṉmiyum Ippaṭikku vaḷḷalār Eḻuttu Ippaṭikku Aḻakapperumāḷ nampi
Eḻuttu Ippaṭikku tevarkaṉmi poṉṉappaṭṭar Eḻuttu Ippaṭikku Abhimuttiśvaramuṭaiyāṉ Eḻuttu Ipramāṇam Eḻutiṉeṉ koyiṟ kaṇakku
perumāṇṭāṉ mayileṟuvāṉāṉa brahmapriyaṉ Eḻuttu §
Digital edition of SII 25.124 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
